example: Deciding on a clear through line is an essential part of developing a strong talk.
zh: 主線，貫穿整個演講的核心思想

example: Another important piece is picking a presentation plan.
zh: 演講計畫，準備和呈現演講的方式
ja: プレゼンテーション計画

example: One way is to write out the talk in full as a complete script.
Imagine you get to watch a friend trying to memorize his talk over the course of a week.
These are clues that the talk is being recited rather than spoken with meaning.
He has the passion he had on day one.
What kind of language do you want to use, spoken language or written language?
zh: 口語，說話時使用的語言

example: The language we use in everyday speech is different from the language writers use.
zh: 書面語，書寫時使用的語言

example: Using elegant language like that you might find in a book is the perfect way of expressing their idea.
I was particularly terrified of speaking up because I had a speech impediment.
What was the point in trying to mumble these thoughts in my head?
Amanda's powerful poetry makes us feel we are in the hands of a masterful writer.
Make eye contact with your audience members.
Some speakers choose to give unscripted talks.
Some speakers use a set of notes to guide them.
Unscripted talks often sound fresh and alive.
It's important to know the difference between unscripted and unprepared.
A lot will depend on what type of journey you plan to take the audience on.
Go back to the metaphor of a talk as a journey.
Create a label for each step to help you remember.
Avoid letting your talk sound robotic.
Pick a presentation plan that you feel confident about.
Deliver your talk passionately.
Spoken words offer something that printed words can't, the human voice.
